title: Exceptions, Diagnostics, Exit Status, and Recovery · Runtime Lens: Recovery Guidance
summary: The command catches Exception around the whole program, prints a traceback to stdout, exits zero after failure, hides the path and cause, and leaves a partial report behind. Predict the user decision, input identity, text policy, expected report, failure boundary, and evidence that could disprove the proposed implementation before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the recovery guidance increment in causal error and recovery contract. Learners progress through predic…
objectives: Give a bounded next action, preserve original inputs, and distinguish retryable from policy or data failures.
Render before replace or write through an atomic strategy when a report file must not become partially valid.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-bookbot-python cumulative Unicode-safe text-analysis portfolio.

Browser execution uses original fixed in-memory fixtures and deterministic pure-Python models only.
Host files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, package installation, native profiling, load,
operating-system races, publication, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class TextPolicy:
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    normalization: str = "NFC"
    casefold: bool = True
    top_k: int = 10


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    corpus_id: str
    revision: str
    policy_version: str
    command_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Bookbot portfolio ready"

content.code:
# Worked focus: bookbot-recovery-guidance — Give a bounded next action, preserve original inputs, and distinguish retryable from policy or data failures.
# Evidence: bookbot-worked-bookbot-errors-exit-status-2
# Competency: bookbot-worked-bookbot-errors-exit-status-2.
# Case 7417: an assistive-technology terminal review.
# Operating constraint: record the first failed boundary, causal evidence, repair, regression test, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk owner.
# Changed case: replace an ASCII word with a canonically equivalent combining sequence and compare the declared counts.
def worked_bookbot_errors_exit_status_2(expected_failures: int = 2, mapped_failures: int = 2, exit_status: int = 2, partial_output: bool = False, recovery_named: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked2 = "worked2-2dekrow-7417"
    assert evidence_variant_worked2
    evidence_axes_worked2 = (
        "corpusworked2",
        "policy2dekrow",
        "revision7417",
        "commandworked27417",
        "report2dekrow7417",
        "test7417worked2",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked2)) == 6
    if expected_failures < 0 or mapped_failures < 0:
        return False, mapped_failures, "failure-count"
    if mapped_failures != expected_failures:
        return False, mapped_failures, "unmapped-error"
    if exit_status == 0:
        return False, exit_status, "false-success"
    if partial_output or not recovery_named:
        return False, exit_status, "unsafe-recovery"
    assert exit_status > 0
    return True, exit_status, "causal-failure"

content.code:
# Evidence: bookbot-recovery-guidance-1h3tie
# Competency: bookbot-recovery-guidance.
# Case 2177: an offline public-library reading report.
# Operating constraint: keep browser work pure, deterministic, bounded, and free of host file, process, Git, package-install, credential, and release effects.
# Changed case: split one word and one UTF-8 sequence across stream chunks and compare batch output byte-for-byte.
def evidence_bookbot_recovery_guidance_1h3tie(expected_failures: int = 2, mapped_failures: int = 2, exit_status: int = 2, partial_output: bool = False, recovery_named: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_1h3tie = "1h3tie-eit3h1-2177"
    assert evidence_variant_1h3tie
    evidence_axes_1h3tie = (
        "corpus1h3tie",
        "policyeit3h1",
        "revision2177",
        "command1h3tie2177",
        "reporteit3h12177",
        "test21771h3tie",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_1h3tie)) == 6
    if expected_failures < 0 or mapped_failures < 0:
        return False, mapped_failures, "failure-count"
    if mapped_failures != expected_failures:
        return False, mapped_failures, "unmapped-error"
    if exit_status == 0:
        return False, exit_status, "false-success"
    if partial_output or not recovery_named:
        return False, exit_status, "unsafe-recovery"
    assert exit_status > 0
    return True, exit_status, "causal-failure"

content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Give a bounded next action, preserve original inputs, and distinguish retryable from policy or data failures.

hints: For Bookbot and Python Defend the Recovery Guidance model · Theory Recovery Guidance, name the stakeholder, corpus authority, revision, path, bytes, encoding, Unicode, token, count, rank, report, command, test, package, release, and transfer boundary, Recovery Guidance precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Bookbot and Python Defend the Recovery Guidance model · Theory Recovery Guidance, trace this concrete input and predict admission, decoding, normalization, tokenization, counting, ranking, formatting, stdout, stderr, status, test, package, failure, and recovery evidence before reviewing it: A theory Bookbot team handles case 6751 during an assistive-technology terminal review. Build deterministic Python evidence to map expected failures to causal diagnostics, stable nonzero status, atomic output, and recovery guidance; record the first failed boundary, causal evidence, repair, regression test, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk owner; then install the built wheel in a fresh environment and rerun success, invalid-argument, and decode-failure cases. Browser code uses pure bounded functions and original fixed fixtures only. Real files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, packages, installations, native profiling, operating-system races, load, publication, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Give a bounded next action, preserve original inputs, and distinguish retryable from policy or data failures.

For Bookbot and Python Defend the Recovery Guidance model · Theory Recovery Guidance, challenge the documented misconception with changed-file, changed-encoding, Unicode, token, ranking, stream, CLI, test, package, security, accessibility, failure, recovery, and controlled native-transfer evidence: Retrying every file failure is a safe recovery plan.

checks.pattern: # Evidence: bookbot-recovery-guidance-1h3tie(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?def\s+evidence_bookbot_recovery_guidance_1h3tie\s*\((?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?\)\s*(?:->[^:]+)?\s*:(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?mapped_failures != expected_failures)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?exit_status == 0)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?partial_output)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?not recovery_named)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?assert)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return)(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return
